skill.name: openai-docs-skill
skill.description: Query the OpenAI developer documentation via the OpenAI Docs MCP server using CLI (curl/jq). Use whenever a task involves the OpenAI API (Responses, Chat Completions, Realtime, etc.), OpenAI SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, Codex, MCP integrations, endpoint schemas, parameters, limits, or migrations and you need up-to-date official guidance.
skill.summary: Search and fetch official OpenAI developer documentation from a shell wrapper.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command substitutions, network calls, filesystem redirects, and two weak-cryptography alerts. Review found the command substitutions are structured jq/curl usage, the filesystem hits are benign redirects, and the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives. The remaining publication risk is medium because the skill intentionally sends queries and document URLs to a configurable external MCP endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Documentation Queries to Configurable Endpoint
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The shell wrapper posts user queries, requested documentation URLs, anchors, endpoint URLs, and language choices to MCP_URL with curl. The default endpoint is an OpenAI developer domain, but MCP_URL can be overridden by the environment, so users should avoid sensitive queries and review endpoint configuration. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The outbound network behavior and endpoint override are explicit in the script, but the observed intent is documentation retrieval rather than malicious exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The outbound network behavior and endpoint override are explicit in the script, but the observed intent is documentation retrieval rather than malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Structured CLI Usage
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for command-injection intent: the flagged substitutions invoke fixed tools such as jq and helper functions, with user input passed as jq arguments or validated numeric values. No evidence found of eval, dynamic shell construction, or execution of attacker-controlled command names. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The relevant command names are static and quoted across the reviewed locations, although the script still depends on external binaries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant command names are static and quoted across the reviewed locations, although the script still depends on external binaries.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Findings Are Benign Redirects
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the static filesystem alerts correspond to standard output redirection to /dev/null while checking command availability and jq error presence. No evidence found of reading user files, writing persistence files, or traversing local directories. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines only discard command output and do not access sensitive filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines only discard command output and do not access sensitive filesystem paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the flagged SKILL.md lines contain skill metadata and documentation URLs, not cryptographic functions, hashing logic, or deprecated algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography being used by this skill. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed lines are plain Markdown instructions and examples with no executable cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are plain Markdown instructions and examples with no executable cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configurable Outbound HTTP Request
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses curl to POST JSON-RPC payloads to MCP_URL, which defaults to an OpenAI developer endpoint but can be changed through the environment. This is expected functionality, but it is a sensitive pattern because query text and requested URLs leave the local machine. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The data flow from command arguments to JSON payloads and curl is visible in the script, with legitimate documentation lookup context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from command arguments to JSON payloads and curl is visible in the script, with legitimate documentation lookup context.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Supplied URL Forwarded to Remote Tool
dangerous_patterns.description: The fetch and openapi subcommands package user-provided URLs into MCP tool calls. This is part of the intended docs workflow, but users should treat queried URLs as information disclosed to the configured MCP service. Confidence: 0.76. Confidence reasoning: The script clearly forwards URL arguments, but there is no evidence that it fetches arbitrary local files or contacts hidden destinations itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly forwards URL arguments, but there is no evidence that it fetches arbitrary local files or contacts hidden destinations itself.

content.value_statement: OpenAI platform details change often, and model memory can become stale. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a shell workflow for searching and fetching official OpenAI documentation.
actual_capabilities: Searches the OpenAI developer documentation through the MCP search tool.
Fetches markdown content for a selected OpenAI documentation URL or anchor.
Lists available OpenAI documentation pages and API endpoints.
Retrieves OpenAPI endpoint schemas and optional code examples.
Requires curl and jq to build requests and format responses.
limitations: Requires network access to the configured MCP endpoint.
Depends on curl and jq being available in the shell environment.
Only covers content exposed by the OpenAI Docs MCP server.
May disclose search terms and requested URLs to the configured endpoint.

use_cases.title: Verify API Parameters
use_cases.description: Check current OpenAI endpoint parameters before writing or reviewing integration code.

use_cases.title: Prepare Migration Guidance
use_cases.description: Fetch official migration pages when moving from older OpenAI APIs to newer patterns.

use_cases.title: Answer Documentation Questions
use_cases.description: Ground assistant responses in current OpenAI documentation and include the source URL.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Documentation Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenAI docs skill to search for the official documentation page about the Responses API. Summarize the best result and include the URL.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Current Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenAI docs skill to fetch the official page for migrating to the Responses API. Summarize the required code changes in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Check Endpoint Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenAI docs skill to list relevant API endpoints for file uploads. Identify the current endpoint names and important request fields.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Schema Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenAI docs skill to fetch the OpenAPI details for a target endpoint. Compare language examples and note any parameters that affect production reliability.

output_examples.input: Search for current OpenAI Realtime API documentation.
output_examples.output: A short summary of the most relevant official documentation page, the source URL, and notes about key capabilities.

output_examples.input: Fetch a migration guide and explain what changed.
output_examples.output: A concise migration summary with current terminology, affected endpoints, and source-backed next steps.
A grouped overview of endpoint documentation that helps select the right page before implementation.

best_practices: Use search before fetch when the exact documentation URL is unknown.
Include the documentation URL in final answers so users can verify the source.
Avoid sending secrets, private prompts, or customer data in documentation queries.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on memory for OpenAI API details when current docs are required.
Do not override MCP_URL unless the alternate endpoint is trusted.
Do not treat fetched examples as production code without reviewing error handling.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a shell workflow for searching and fetching official OpenAI developer documentation through an MCP server.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The script sends requests to the configured OpenAI Docs MCP endpoint.

faq.question: Does it execute arbitrary commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The script calls fixed tools such as curl and jq, and it quotes user input.

faq.question: Can I change the MCP endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes. The MCP_URL environment variable can override the default endpoint, but only trusted endpoints should be used.

faq.question: What should not be sent through this skill?
faq.answer: Do not send secrets, private customer data, unreleased code names, or confidential prompts in search terms or URLs.
